title: 1.1: Tests that fletcher16_checksum returns an integer checksum value when given byte input
1.2: Tests that fletcher16_checkbytes returns bytes when given input buffer and offset
1.3: ests that hexdiff produces diff output without crashing when comparing different inputs including mixed type combinations (e.g., bytes vs. packet)
1.4: Tests that corrupt_bytes modifies at least one byte in the input
1.5: Tests that corrupt_bits flips at least one bit in the input
2.1: Places the Fletcher-16 checksum test in the test/ directory
2.2: Places the hexdiff test in the test/ directory
2.3: Places the data corruption test in the test/ directory
3.1: All tests use the UTscapy test framework format with = prefix for test names
3.2: All tests use assert statements for verification
4.1: Follows the pattern of using ContextManagerCaptureOutput to capture stdout for print-based utility functions like hexdiff
4.2: Follows the pattern of setting random.seed() for reproducibility in tests involving randomness
